reachability_artifacts: docs/release/2026-06-19-safe-parallelism/S61-cli-output-styling/proof.md — terminal transcript showing SWORN_FORCE_COLOR=1 sworn version|help|top emitting ANSI (escape counts 2/32/2) and NO_COLOR=1 emitting zero escapes (0/0/0)
go test ./cmd/sworn/... (styled commands exercised through entry points, 117/118 pass — 1 pre-existing TestCmdRun_Parallel failure)

verification.rework_notes: Prior round failed_verification with 3 violations: (1) sworn help emitted 0 ANSI under SWORN_FORCE_COLOR=1 because usage() used no style helpers; (2) cmd/sworn/init.go was a planned touchpoint but never styled (26 fmt.Print* stdout calls); (3) proof.md Reachability artefact had no terminal transcript. Re-entry addresses all three: styling usage() in main.go, styling init.go user-facing stdout, and regenerating proof.md with a live terminal transcript.
prior_violations: AC3: sworn help emits 0 ANSI escapes with SWORN_FORCE_COLOR=1 — usage() (cmd/sworn/main.go:96-162) uses no style helpers; spec requires sworn version|help|top to emit ANSI under force-color

Gate 2: cmd/sworn/init.go is a planned touchpoint but NOT in the diff; init.go has 26 fmt.Print* stdout calls with no style import; proof 'Divergence from plan' falsely claims init.go 'writes only to stderr'

Gate 4: proof.md 'Reachability artefact' contains no terminal transcript showing SWORN_FORCE_COLOR=1 sworn version|help|top ANSI output + NO_COLOR=1 zero-escape runs (spec Required tests explicitly demands this transcript)
verifier_observations: PASS — all six gates satisfied. All three prior violations genuinely resolved: (1) sworn help now emits 32 ANSI escapes under SWORN_FORCE_COLOR=1 (usage() refactored to styled strings.Builder writing to os.Stderr); (2) init.go now imports internal/style and emits 6 escapes under force-color (was 0), plain output byte-identical (sha256 d5b8d0d4... matches base); (3) proof.md Reachability artefact contains a real terminal transcript with escape counts matching live binary runs (version 2/0, help 32/0, top 2/0).

AC1: 11 style helpers present, Enabled() semantics tested (NoColor/ForceColor/DisabledReturnsPlain). AC2: sworn help + init byte-identical to base under NO_COLOR (help sha 81c36dcf...). AC3: version 2/0, help 32/0, top 2/0 (force/no-color). AC4: pad-then-style confirmed in init.go:124,136 and ears.go:341. AC5: go.mod unchanged. AC6: go build + go vet both exit 0.

TestCmdRun_Parallel fails (117/118 cmd/sworn tests pass) — confirmed pre-existing on release-wt base commit (same exit 2, 'implementer model not configured'), environmental, out of slice scope, excluded from verdict.

Minor non-blocking observation: gofmt -l flags 11 changed files (e.g. style.go missing trailing newline, bench.go )+ spacing). These are cosmetic formatting nits, not a spec AC violation (AC6 is build+vet only, both pass). proof.md's 'gofmt clean' claim is accurate for the 3 files it checked (main.go/init.go/telemetry.go) but inaccurate for 11 others. A `gofmt -w .` would fix without touching logic; does not block merge.

design_decisions.human_decision: acknowledged — reference branch code is clean and tested; copy verbatim
design_decisions.rationale: Spec directs reuse; reference package is zero-dependency and already passes style_test.go

design_decisions.choice: Command-file styling wraps existing fmt.Print* arguments in style.*() calls, never rephrases
design_decisions.human_decision: acknowledged — wrapping pattern guarantees byte-identical plain-text output
design_decisions.rationale: Preserves all golden-string and table-alignment tests

design_decisions.choice: main.go touchpoints: usage(), cmdVersion(), cmdHelp() only; skip dispatch() and TUI path
design_decisions.human_decision: acknowledged — T15 owns dispatch/registry pattern; no output text to style there
design_decisions.rationale: Avoids cross-track collision with T15 command registry

design_decisions.choice: Divider runs of = stay Dim-wrapped rather than converted to style.Rule
design_decisions.human_decision: acknowledged — preserves byte-identical plain output; Rule() emits box-drawing chars
design_decisions.rationale: Spec Risk section explicitly calls this out

design_decisions.choice: usage() text block: Banner at top, hint line at bottom; middle prose stays plain
design_decisions.human_decision: acknowledged — over-styling reference material degrades readability
